post.title: Your First 5K Challenge
post.body: 1d  • Product Help DiscussionYour First 5K ChallengeOn Day 4 of the Y5K Challenge and I’m mind blown. So many gems dropped, but my question is I know Arlan has always recommended YouTube as one of the best social media platforms, but I was just wondering thoughts on Facebook? I’m monetized, and the majority of my clients come from there. Should I do both or focus on one?

comment.author: Krystale Janel
comment.text: @Daniel Carter thank you so much Daniel!

thread.author: Jarricka Worthy
thread.text: Arlan highly recommends YT, she also drilled home being consistent on your platform of choice in this challenge. Which platform would you be consistent on?

thread.author: Krystale Janel
thread.text: @Jarricka Worthy I’m consistent right now on Facebook, but wondering based on the challenge should I start with YouTube. I recently found out my Facebook is now monetized as well.

thread.author: Jarricka Worthy
thread.text: @Krystale Janel congratulations that’s a big deal ❤️! Based on Arlan’s newest book, now is the time to start on YT. Even if you post once a week. You may even find that you have some FB content that you can repurpose for YT

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: Congrats Krystale. . . pumpin' out that strong energy. Keep it up.

thread.author: Krystale Janel
thread.text: @Daniel Carter thank you so much Daniel!

post.author: Naya Stone
post.title: honest question: how do you navigate the space between burnout and belief?
post.body: 2d  • General discussionhonest question: how do you navigate the space between burnout and belief?I’m still committed to what i’m building, but i’m in a tight season mentally and financially.I'm not looking to quit nor am I really in a position to, just trying to understand how others have moved through this phase.Thoughts, personal stories, welcome. 🙏

comment.author: Naya Stone
comment.text: @Daniel Carter That line about not tying the quality of our daily life to business performance is a great reminder. Thank you for sharing that. 🙏

thread.author: Janet Nambi
thread.text: I have been there, almost 6 years ago. I know exactly how you feel. Give yourself permission to be in the messy middle. Don’t push anything, don’t force anything. Focus on small actions each day and celebrate each accomplished one. If you feel like you still need to rediscover yourself or reconnect with your calmer self or healed self, take each day at a time. I learned the hard way when I tried to force action and results while my body and souls cried for calm. I pray you find your way out of i

thread.author: Dk Harris
thread.text: @Janet Nambi Great advice.

thread.author: Naya Stone
thread.text: @Janet Nambi thank you for this. “messy middle” feels very accurate right now.I think that’s been part of my challenge, trying to create momentum while also feeling deeply tired. learning where discipline ends and forcing begins has been…a lesson.I really appreciate your words 🙏. and I’m glad you made it through your own season.

thread.author: Chidera Ugwuanyi
thread.text: What usually helps is separating two things..."your belief vs your capacity".Your belief might still be strong…but your *energy, time, or finances* might be low.Burnout often isn’t “I don’t believe anymore”it’s “I don’t have the capacity to keep pushing the same way.”So the move isn’t to quit…it’s to adjust the pace without dropping the direction.Sometimes that looks like...showing up smaller, but still showing upfocusing on fewer things, but doing them bettergiving yourself a system instead of

thread.author: Milan Perry
thread.text: @Chidera Ugwuanyi extremely powerful!! All of it is so true and on point. I just came to realize this being a dementia caregiver and trying to restart my online Wellness & Lifestyle business. It wasn't working at the same level because I was working at a different frequency now and it became important for me to respect that

thread.author: Naya Stone
thread.text: @Chidera Ugwuanyi This really hit, especially “your belief vs your capacity.” that’s exactly the tension i’ve been feeling and haven’t had language for.My belief is very much intact, but trying to operate at full speed under current circumstances has been catching up to me.“adjust the pace without dropping the direction” feels like the lesson here. Thank you for the great advice!

thread.author: Genevieve Horrall
thread.text: First thing @Naya Stone send you a virtual hug. Man have I been at this crossroads more than once. Simplicity and simplifying things have been key for me.Here have been some of my biggest take aways from having navigating these moments:Realize this is a moment - take a breath and know it's okay to show up differently right now. No permission needed. Get a pen and paper and write out what is not working, then write out what is working - don't over think it, just let it flow and be messy.You get t

thread.author: Naya Stone
thread.text: @Genevieve Horrall thank you for this, truly! And receiving that very much needed virtual hug. 💛“you get to decide what you can hold right now” really landed. I think I’ve been trying to carry everything at once because the pressure feels very real, but that approach clearly isn’t sustainable.Also very guilty of being bad at 5 and 6 😅 learning. I really appreciate this.

thread.author: Naya Stone
thread.text: Really grateful for everyone who shared their perspective here.I’ll be honest, vulnerability when I’m actively struggling is not easy for me 🫣. I usually prefer to process privately and return when things feel polished.But this conversation reminded me how many people are quietly carrying hard seasons while still trying to build something meaningful.If this helped anyone else feel a little less alone in their own process, I’m glad I shared it.✨

thread.author: Hil Kane
thread.text: @Naya Stone I'm glad you shared too, for *you*! that's what the community is for!

thread.author: Leah Mamabolo
thread.text: Let's hold on we are together hoping life will change soon

thread.author: Crystal Green
thread.text: I appreciate this conversation because I can relate. The last 3 months or so have been going well but for most of last year I was in a really tough season. I’m glad people in this community are so willing to discuss even the hard things and to be here for each other and support and encourage one another. It’s a beautiful thing. Thank you for sharing @Naya Stone.

thread.author: Naya Stone
thread.text: @Crystal Green Thank you for sharing this. And I’m really glad things have been going well these past few months, especially after such a tough season.🙌I agree, I’ve been genuinely surprised (in a good way) by how open and supportive this community has been. It made sharing something I normally would’ve kept private feel worth it.Appreciate you being part of the conversation!

thread.author: Daniel Carter
thread.text: The Tao reminds us: In spring the trees blossom but we can't force them to grow faster. Ideas and new approaches for business are exactly the same. I think it's also very important that the quality of our daily life is not determined by how well or not well the business is doing. . .

thread.author: Naya Stone
thread.text: @Daniel Carter That line about not tying the quality of our daily life to business performance is a great reminder. Thank you for sharing that. 🙏

thread.author: Wanda M Jackson
thread.text: This thread is full of amazing advice. Thank you for posting @Naya Stone and everyone that shared. This truly helped me analyze where I am and how to navigate to the other side. I've learned in my own journey breathing isn't just the breath you take but it's also about the depth of the breath.

Today we completed Day 1 and have solidified our offers. We’re so excited! I’ve been watching Arlan for some time and finally decided to do what I’ve been wanting to do—I just wasn’t sure where to start. I’ve created so much, and I know it’s time to get it out there. I can’t wait until it’s completed—no turning back!!! 🙌❤️‍🔥🔥👏
